blocks.html: Sometimes code hits a problem — dividing by zero, a missing file, bad data. Python calls this an error (or "exception"). Without help, an error crashes your whole program.

blocks.starter:
print("Before the error")
result = 10 / 0
print("This line never runs")
blocks.label: Run Code

blocks.html: try / except lets you catch an error and handle it gracefully instead of crashing.

blocks.label: Run Code

blocks.html: Different mistakes raise different error types. ValueError happens when you convert text that isn't a valid number.

blocks.label: Run Code

blocks.html: Write a try/except block that attempts int("abc") and catches the ValueError, printing a friendly message instead of crashing.
